accepted.reason: Dense key/value tag parsing and tag-column explosion provide structured loop-heavy dict/list transformations.
Class reassignment local-search over matrix-like state is a strong iterative optimization workload.
CSR Gauss-Seidel and projected updates are classic numerical kernels suited for Cython speedups.
Correlation Jacobian/Hessian computations are dense numeric loops with clear optimization opportunities.
Iterative volatility solve and rating updates provide nontrivial math-heavy control flow for optimization.

skipped.reason: Mostly declarations/utility scaffolding with limited standalone kernel content.
Interpreter behavior/string-iteration test fixture, not representative optimization workload.
General math helper collection without a focused end-to-end kernel/problem statement.
Marked BROKEN/toremove and tied to external project types; not suitable for clean training examples.
High overlap with existing reindex-style unpaired data already present; skipped to reduce duplication.
